title: Diagnosing a DynamoDB Hot Partition
description:
## Throttling Despite Plenty of Capacity

A DynamoDB table stores IoT telemetry. The team provisioned far more capacity than the table's average consumed throughput, yet they see frequent **`ProvisionedThroughputExceededException` (throttling)** on writes. Investigation shows the partition key is `device_type`, and there are only a handful of device types — so almost all traffic lands on a few partition key values. The team needs a durable fix that lets the table use its capacity evenly across partitions.

Which change BEST resolves the throttling?

options.text: Redesign the table to use a high-cardinality partition key (e.g. device_id, or a composite key) so requests spread evenly across many partitions
Keep the schema and simply raise the provisioned write capacity units (WCU) substantially
Migrate the data to Amazon RDS, which does not have partitions
Enable DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) to absorb the write traffic

explanation: Throttling here is caused by a **hot partition**: a low-cardinality partition key (`device_type`) concentrates traffic onto a few partitions, so a small number of keys exhaust their per-partition share of throughput while the table as a whole is under-utilized. The durable fix is to choose a **high-cardinality partition key** (e.g. `device_id`) or a composite/sharded key so load distributes evenly — DynamoDB scales by spreading items across many partitions and only performs well when keys are well-distributed. **Raising WCU** (B) is wasteful and often ineffective because the bottleneck is one partition, not aggregate capacity (adaptive capacity helps but won't fully cure a severely skewed key). **Moving to RDS** (C) abandons the right tool over a fixable data-model issue. **DAX** (D) is a read-through/write-through cache that accelerates **reads**, not writes — it does not solve write throttling from a skewed key.

hints.title: Aggregate vs per-key
hints.body: If total capacity is high but you still throttle, the problem is distribution across partition keys, not the total.

hints.title: What DAX accelerates
hints.body: DAX is a caching layer aimed at read latency/offload. Ask whether the problem is reads or writes.
